steps.title: Connect the bird to silence
steps.content: The friend says his soul feels calm because there is no strife. The speaker then describes a kestrel muting its mating note to suit the "sweet silence," so the bird reflects the same peaceful quiet the people experience.

steps.title: Reject unsupported functions
steps.content: A adds mystery about nature that the poem does not suggest. B says the friend hides his feelings, but the text says the friends understand each other. D claims the definition of peace changes, while the poem emphasizes shared harmony. The answer is C.
